page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a-nps1.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ecs.c7a family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.55 GHz. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-optimized allocation of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In performance benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% of tested servers with a stress-ng single-core score of 2697.59. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 43208.77 and a Passmark CPU score of 38324.48. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show average throughput, registering 2067.14 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 83.67 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform within average parameters, as evidenced by a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 144.85 GB/sec and a Redis SET throughput of 30.6 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores relative to memory, making it a resource-efficient option for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff that lowers baseline resource overhead. This profile is suitable for high-compute tasks, batch processing, web serving, and distributed microservices.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a-nps1.8xlarge is a compute-optimized x86_64 server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at 2.55 GHz. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs paired with 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, including a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 144.85 GB/sec and 30.6 million Redis SET operations per second. This hardware profile delivers qualitative resource density for compute-heavy applications. It is well-suited for web serving, batch processing, and general compute workloads.
